operating_state: Total revenue increased 16% to $143.8 billion driven by iPhone growth from higher Pro model sales and Services expansion from advertising, App Store and cloud services. Regional performance was broadly positive with double-digit growth across most markets while Greater China surged and Japan grew modestly. Mac and Wearables declined, partially offsetting iPhone and Services momentum.
cost_structure: Total gross margin expanded to 48.2% from 46.9% driven by favorable product mix despite tariff cost headwinds, with Products margin improving and Services margin rising. Operating expenses increased from R&D growth driven by infrastructure investments, headcount expansion and engineering program costs, while SG&A rose from headcount and variable selling expense increases.
material_events: The company repurchased $25.0 billion of common stock and paid dividends during the quarter. Product launches included updated MacBook Pro, iPad Pro, and Apple Vision Pro models. Annual shareholder meeting approved standard governance matters while rejecting a China entanglement audit proposal.
macro_exposures: New tariffs on imports from China, India, Japan, South Korea, Taiwan, Vietnam and EU created material cost pressures, with reciprocal tariffs imposed by several countries. Section 232 semiconductor investigation published initial results with no immediate additional tariffs but further measures remain possible. Foreign currency revaluation regulations increased effective tax rate to 17.5% from 14.7%.
forward_outlook: Management intends to increase dividends annually subject to board approval and maintains authorized share repurchase program without minimum acquisition requirements. Manufacturing purchase obligations total $44.4 billion with most due within 12 months, while other purchase obligations reach $35.1 billion.
uncertainty_profile: Pending accounting standard adoptions include internal-use software capitalization changes, income statement expense disaggregation requirements, and enhanced income tax disclosures over the next three years. Trade dispute escalation and additional tariff measures remain unresolved with uncertain magnitude and duration impacts on supply chain, component availability and pricing.
